Name: Rose Rocketry
Relevance: Umbrella organization for the institute's multiple high-power and liquid rocketry programs

Name: Rose Propulsion Laboratory (RPL)
Relevance: Focused on the development of liquid-fuel rocket engines and propulsive landing vehicles for the CPLC

Name: Rose-Hulman Electric Propulsion Group (RHEPG)
Relevance: First undergraduate team to ignite a gridded ion thruster and currently researching heaterless hollow cathodes

Name: NASA Cube Satellite Launch Initiative (SmallSat)
Relevance: Develops CubeSats with star shade technology for LEO observational astronomy and ground station infrastructure

Name: NASA University Student Launch Initiative (USLI) Team
Relevance: Builds and launches high-power rockets and scientific payloads in partnership with NASA Marshall Space Flight Center

Name: Hobby Rocketry
Relevance: Entry-level program for high-power rocketry certification and independent student research projects

Name: Mars Rover Team
Relevance: Designs and operates Martian rovers for extreme terrain navigation and autonomous science missions

Name: NASA Lunar Autonomy Challenge Team
Relevance: Top-10 national finalist team developing autonomous navigation and computer vision software for lunar exploration

Name: Design/Build/Fly (DBF)
Relevance: Designs and builds remote-controlled aircraft and drones for international AIAA flight competitions

Name: Oakley Observatory
Relevance: Research-grade facility for minor planet discovery asteroid photometry and variable star research

Name: Rose-Hulman Astronomical Society
Relevance: Student organization managing the Oakley Observatory and hosting astronomical research events

Name: Aeronautics and Astronautics
Relevance: Student interest group focused on aeronautical engineering and space science professional development

Name: MiNDS Cleanroom (Micro-Nanoscale Device and Systems)
Relevance: Facility for fabrication of semiconductors MEMS and nanotechnology for aerospace electronics

Name: Center for Applied Optics Studies (CAOS)
Relevance: Research center focused on optical engineering laser systems and photonic technologies

Name: Rose-Hulman Ventures
Relevance: Assists startups and industry partners in prototyping aerospace hardware such as security drones and antenna systems

Name: Robotics Workspace Committee
Relevance: Oversees multidisciplinary robotics projects including the Mars Rover and RoboMaster competition teams

Name: Mechanical Engineering Aerospace Concentration
Relevance: Academic track providing specialized courses in propulsion aerodynamics and helicopter engine labs

Name: Ultrashort Pulse Laser Laboratory
Relevance: Research facility focusing on high-energy laser systems in collaboration with naval research entities

Name: Southern Sky Observatory
Relevance: Remote research partnership providing access to telescopes in the Southern Hemisphere

Name: Rose-Hulman SEDS Chapter
Relevance: Local chapter of Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (Active/Historical winner)

Name: AIAA Student Branch
Relevance: Professional student branch of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics

Name: Helicopter Engine Lab
Relevance: Instructional lab utilizing a gas turbine helicopter engine for propulsion and thermodynamics study

Name: Moonwatch Club
Relevance: Historic 1960s program at Rose Polytechnic for monitoring and tracking orbital satellites

Name: Terre Haute Astronomical Society
Relevance: Historic affiliate group that collaborated with Rose-Hulman on early satellite tracking initiatives

Name: Lynn H. Reeder Laboratory and Observatory
Relevance: Historical facility (pre-Oakley) dedicated to early space-age satellite and planetary research

Name: Independent Project/Research Opportunities Program (IP/ROP)
Relevance: Institutional program that funds advanced research such as the Electric Propulsion Group

Name: MATE Underwater Robotics
Relevance: Develops ROVs for missions that simulate the autonomy and telemetry needs of space exploration

Name: Non-Linear Lab
Relevance: Conducts research on optical materials and devices critical for advanced sensing and communications

Name: Optical Communication Lab
Relevance: Dedicated facility for studying quantum optics and laser-based space communications

Name: Characterization Lab
Relevance: Provides material analysis for aerospace components using SEM and X-ray diffraction

Name: High-Altitude Balloon Project
Relevance: Interdisciplinary student initiative to test CubeSat hardware and sensors at near-space altitudes

Name: RoboMaster Team
Relevance: Develops fleets of multi-subsystem robots utilizing advanced computer vision and autonomy algorithms

Name: VEX U Team
Relevance: Multidisciplinary team developing custom robots for competition focusing on advanced sensors and control

Name: Combat Robotics
Relevance: Develops beetleweight and antweight robots focusing on structural durability and high-impact mechanics

Name: Chem-E-Car
Relevance: Builds small unmanned cars powered by chemical mechanisms related to autonomous propulsion research

Name: Department of Physics and Optical Engineering
Relevance: Departmental research focused on high-energy astrophysics plasma physics and orbital mechanics

Name: Branam and Kremer Innovation Centers (BIC/KIC)
Relevance: The physical nexus of the institute's aerospace competition teams and specialized fabrication labs
